betterquesting:10.desc:8:
The Isolator will take Ethanol and Enzyme to extract a single gene from a candidate bee. Sometimes it gets hungry and eats the bee too. Don't feed it any bees you won't mind getting destroyed!

You must provide a Blank Sequence to store the output gene. This will be stored with a "faint" level of strength that can be sequenced slowly, or made stronger to be sequenced faster by passing the sequence through a Polymerizer.

The exact gene you get from the bee is going to be a random choice from that bee's genome. You won't know what it is until you run it through the Sequencer. It will then be accessible forever in the Gene Database.

You can wind up isolating the same gene from the same bee multiple times. It's just bad luck. They won't be isolated in any particular order.

The Sequencer will drain the container back into a Blank Sequence, so you can feed them back into the Isolator. You only ever need to make a few Blank Sequences because you can just loop like this indefinitely. If, however, you have a sequenced gene and want to make it blank again immediately, then just run it through your smelting/furnace device of choice.
